content.text:
<user_query>
front end deployment failed chore: forensic reconciliation of remaining calibration chat commits … #231
</user_query>

I’m going to trace the deployment failure first, then map it to the `#231` reconciliation work so we can make a targeted fix. I’ll start by reading the terminal logs you already had open and then inspect the frontend build path.
I found only partial PR metadata in those logs, not the actual deployment stack trace, so I’m now checking this worktree directly and reproducing the frontend build failure locally.
I couldn’t find the exact `#231` phrase in source, so I’m moving to direct failure reproduction: identify the frontend deploy/build command used in CI, run it here, and patch whatever breaks.
I’ve located the CI workflow that builds the frontend image; next I’m inspecting `Dockerfile.frontend` and then running the same frontend build command to capture the exact failing error locally.
I’m now running the frontend production build (`craco build`) exactly as CI does, so we can capture the concrete compile/deploy blocker instead of guessing.
Local failure is currently just missing installed frontend deps, so I’ll install packages in `frontend/` and rerun build to get the actual code-level error that CI would hit.

I hit a local Node version mismatch (`18` vs required `20`), which is separate from CI. I’ll continue with `--ignore-engines` to at least compile-check this branch and isolate source issues tied to the failed deployment.

The frontend build itself compiles, so the failure is likely in CI/deploy workflow logic or missing reconciliation commits. I’m pulling issue/PR `#231` context from GitHub next to target the exact break.

`#231` doesn’t exist as a GH issue/PR in this repo, so I’m treating it as an internal chore label and now tracing recent calibration-related commits to identify what’s missing from this detached state.

I found a likely target branch named `fix/frontend-deploy-reconcile`, which matches your request wording; I’m diffing it against `main` now to reconcile any missing calibration-chat commits tied to the failed frontend deploy.

I found a key clue: `origin/main` is still on the reconciliation commit, while your local `main` includes newer calibration hotfix commits. I’m checking the failed GitHub Action logs to pinpoint which frontend step broke and whether those newer commits already contain the fix.
I’ve pulled the exact failed run logs now; next I’m extracting the failing step and error lines, then I’ll patch the workflow or code path accordingly.

Root cause is clear from CI: the reconciliation commit introduced duplicated/garbled JS blocks in multiple frontend files. I’m now isolating exactly which later commits repaired those files so we can cleanly reconcile and ship the minimal safe fix set.

I’ve confirmed your local tree already contains a clean branch that compiles, while `origin/main` is on the broken reconciliation commit. I’m now reconstructing a *minimal hotfix on top of the broken commit* so we can preserve intent but remove the syntax-corruption that killed frontend deploy.

I’m now drilling into the exact broken files from the bad commit to reconstruct the intended content (instead of bluntly reverting), so the hotfix preserves calibration-chat behavior while restoring a green frontend build.

I’m going to apply a direct hotfix on top of the broken reconciliation commit (`6d383e91`) by replacing only the syntax-corrupted frontend files with the known-good versions, then rerun the frontend build as verification.

I’ve applied the clean versions of all eight files that produced the CI syntax errors; I’m running the frontend production build on this hotfix branch now to verify the deployment blocker is eliminated.
Build now passes in the app workspace; I’m running a full `Dockerfile.frontend` build (same path as GitHub Actions) to confirm CI parity before I finalize.

Root cause confirmed from failed run `23729957007` (`Build & Deploy BIQc`): the reconciliation commit (`6d383e91`) introduced concatenated/duplicated JS blocks, which broke Docker frontend build at `RUN yarn build` with syntax errors.

I applied a hotfix on a new branch: `fix/frontend-deploy-reconcile-231` (branched from the failing commit), replacing only the corrupted frontend files with known-good versions.

Updated files:
- `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`
- `frontend/src/components/website/WebsiteLayout.js`
- `frontend/src/context/SupabaseAuthContext.js`
- `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`
- `frontend/src/lib/api.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/CalibrationQaAccess.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/MarketPage.js`
- `frontend/src/pages/website/HomePage.js`

Validation:
- `yarn build` in `frontend/` ✅
- Full CI-equivalent image build (`docker build -f Dockerfile.frontend ...`) ✅

Current git state:
- Staged: the 8 hotfix files above
- Unstaged: existing `package-lock.json` change (preserved)

If you want, I can now create a commit and push this branch, then trigger/re-run the deployment workflow.
